principles: Modules are self-contained and register via their ServiceProvider.
Config keys are namespaced under the module name to avoid collisions.
Public assets are exposed via the package Vite bridge (ModularityVite).

register: Bind interfaces to implementations in the register() method.
Merge package/module config using $this->mergeConfigFrom when needed.
Register commands, routes and event listeners via the provider.
boot: Publishable resources must be registered with tags.
Defer heavy work to booted() callbacks when console environment requires it.

structure: Use Inertia pages under Resources/js/Pages or modules/<Name>/Resources/js/Pages
Vuetify components follow composition API and are small, single-responsibility
build: Expose module entrypoints to host Vite build via ModularityVite helper.
Keep CSS scoped at component level and use Vuetify theme tokens.
naming: PascalCase for component filenames and default export names.
Pages as Index.vue or <Resource>.vue inside Pages/ folders.

unit: Prefer mocking external services; test providers by registering them in a Testbench app.
integration: Use Orchestra Testbench to bootstrap package service providers.
Include database migrations/seeders in workbench/ for integration tests.
coverage: Coverage commands generate module-specific coverage reports found in coverage-xml/.
CI should fail PR checks if coverage thresholds are not met (use existing CoveragePRCheckCommand).

workflows: Separate jobs for lint (pint/phpstan), test, and coverage.
Run package tests with composer scripts defined in composer.json.
Upload coverage artifacts and run CoveragePRCheckCommand to enforce thresholds.
